patch.description:
The full patch text. Must begin with '*** Begin Patch' on its own line and end with '*** End Patch' on its own line. Between these markers, include one or more file operations.

All file paths are relative to the project root (never absolute, never start with /).

--- ADD A NEW FILE ---
Use '*** Add File: <path>' followed by lines prefixed with '+'.

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: src/utils/helper.py
+def greet(name):
+    return f"Hello, {name}"
*** End Patch

--- DELETE A FILE ---
Use '*** Delete File: <path>' with no body lines.

*** Begin Patch
*** Delete File: src/old_module.py
*** End Patch

--- UPDATE AN EXISTING FILE ---
Use '*** Update File: <path>' followed by one or more hunks. Each hunk starts with '@@ context_line' to locate where the change goes, then uses line prefixes: ' ' (space) for unchanged context, '-' for lines to remove, '+' for lines to add.

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/config.rs
@@ let timeout = 30;
-let timeout = 30;
+let timeout = 60;
*** End Patch

--- MOVE / RENAME A FILE ---
Use '*** Update File: <old_path>' immediately followed by '*** Move to: <new_path>', then hunks (if content also changes) or no hunks (pure rename not supported; include at least one hunk).

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/old_name.rs
*** Move to: src/new_name.rs
@@ fn old_func()
-fn old_func()
+fn new_func()
*** End Patch

--- MULTI-FILE PATCH ---
Chain multiple operations in a single patch.

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: src/new.rs
+pub fn added() {}
*** Update File: src/lib.rs
@@ mod existing;
+mod new;
*** Delete File: src/deprecated.rs
*** End Patch

--- HUNK MATCHING ---
The '@@ context_line' locates a unique line in the file; search starts from the top (or after the previous hunk). The '-' lines must exactly match consecutive lines in the file at that location. If context or old lines cannot be found, the patch is rejected with an error. Matching tolerates trailing whitespace differences and Unicode normalization (smart quotes -> ASCII, etc.), but the line content must otherwise match.

Multiple hunks in one Update File are applied top-to-bottom. Each hunk's search starts after the previous hunk's match position.

Use '@@' (bare, no text after it) when the first hunk has no context line and the old lines are unambiguous.

Use '*** End of File' after the last hunk's lines to strip the file's trailing newline.

--- COMMON SYNTAX MISTAKES ---
1. Forgetting the space prefix on context lines (every unchanged line MUST start with ' ').
2. Using '@@' with a line number instead of the actual line content. It takes literal text, not a number.
3. Omitting '+' prefix on Add File content lines.
4. Putting absolute paths instead of project-root-relative paths.
5. Including the '*** Begin Patch' / '*** End Patch' markers more than once.
6. Adding blank lines between hunks that get parsed as empty context lines. Blank lines in hunks are treated as empty context (matched against empty lines in the file).

shell.description: Input arguments for the shell tool

background.description: If true, run in the process-local background and return a job ID immediately; current background jobs are volatile and do not survive a process restart

command.description: The command to execute (POSIX-style parsing for policy checks)

timeout_secs.description: Timeout in seconds (uses config default if not specified)

shell.title: ShellInput

shell_job_cancel.description: Input arguments for the job cancel tool
shell_job_cancel.title: JobCancelInput

shell_job_status.description: Input arguments for the job status tool
shell_job_status.title: JobStatusInput

blocked_by.description: IDs of tasks that block THIS task

blocks.description: IDs of tasks that this task blocks

description.description: Detailed description of what needs to be done

TaskStatus.description: Status of a task in its lifecycle

oneOf.description: Task is waiting to be started
Task is actively being worked on

TaskStatus.description: Status of a task in its lifecycle

oneOf.description: Task is waiting to be started
Task is actively being worked on

add_blocked_by.description: Task IDs to add to blocked_by list (optional)

add_blocks.description: Task IDs to add to blocks list (optional)

id.description: The ID of the task to update

metadata.description: Metadata key-value pairs to merge (null value = delete key)

remove_blocked_by.description: Task IDs to remove from blocked_by list (optional)

remove_blocks.description: Task IDs to remove from blocks list (optional)

path.description: Path to the image file to view (relative to project root, or absolute within project).
